findings.finding: Zapier already documents a direct blank-workflow start plus draft preservation, which sets a strong expectation for immediate setup.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS must prove that owner-solo onboarding reaches a safe first preview with equal or lower cognitive load.

findings.finding: n8n exposes strong builder power and human review controls, but through a more technical surface.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should keep advanced capability available behind progressive disclosure instead of making it the baseline.

findings.finding: Salesforce proves testing-center breadth, but ARIS can still be stronger if its preview remains deterministic and shorter to interpret.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS preview must emphasize before-action trust, not only testing infrastructure.

findings.finding: Power Automate has mature approval patterns across desktop and mobile, setting an enterprise baseline for gate coverage.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should keep approvals tighter and more comprehensible than enterprise workflow routing.

findings.finding: Verified approval sources prove that actions can be reviewed, but they do not prove strong owner-facing risk explanation language.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should treat explicit risk explanation as a product pillar and keep a ledger of any future places where it becomes vague.

findings.finding: Airtable proves strong history and audit capability, but not a business receipt that explains what happened in plain language.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should convert trace depth into receipt clarity instead of only mirroring admin logs.

findings.finding: Zapier replay proves rerun mechanics, but not honest language around irreversible effects or compensation.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should preserve its reversible versus compensating language and make it demo-visible.

findings.finding: Make has strong operator history and search, but this packet still lacks equivalent evidence that ARIS failure states are equally inspectable once implemented.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should make degraded states both explainable and searchable, not only visually clear.

findings.finding: Airtable explicitly documents mobile-optimized interfaces and immediate action patterns.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS mobile companion should keep the same immediacy while remaining safer and calmer for decisions.

findings.finding: No current official Make mobile control source strong enough for scoring was verified in this packet.
findings.implication_for_aris: Do not publish mobile hierarchy claims against Make until a current official source is available.

findings.finding: Power Automate already documents lifecycle audit logs, run metadata and admin analytics at runtime scale.
findings.implication_for_aris: If ARIS ever wants enterprise-grade trust, it will need a future runtime audit path beyond candidate-only UX artifacts.

findings.finding: HubSpot already proves audit summaries and Analyze-tab charts, which is stronger than a purely conceptual dashboard packet.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS dashboard work needs measurable actionability rules, not only widget inventory.

findings.finding: Zapier proves connector-level safe testing via test mode, which helps demo without live money.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS sandbox demos should keep the same no-real-side-effect clarity, but compress the story into a more trust-centric script.

findings.finding: Salesforce public positioning is broad and enterprise-heavy, so weakly sourced dashboard claims can easily become theater if ARIS overreacts.
findings.implication_for_aris: ARIS should answer dashboard competition with task clarity and decision depth, not by copying enterprise breadth claims.
